message:
chore: add a.out to gitignore, fix SCP path on Windows, sync beads

- Add a.out (ELF binary) to .gitignore under build artifacts
- Remove Windows backslash conversion in act_run_native_build SCP path
  (OpenSSH SCP uses forward slashes even on Windows)
- Sync beads: 2 new backlog chores filed for test fixture cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -82,12 +82,14 @@
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.5","title":"Runtime guardrails: Bash version + path normalization","description":"# Runtime Guardrails: Bash Version + Path Normalization

## Purpose
Harden dsr runtime behavior to prevent subtle failures (Bash version mismatch, relative path writes, unsafe deletions) and mirror the reliability of `ru`.

## Requirements
- **Bash 4+ gate** with actionable guidance (macOS: brew install bash)
- **Absolute path enforcement** for `--config`, `--state-dir`, `--cache-dir` (accept `~`)
- **Safe temp dirs**: always use `/tmp` or `mktemp -d` (never create in `/data/projects`)
- **Safe delete helper**: allowlist deletions to `DSR_STATE_DIR` + `DSR_CACHE_DIR`
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** disables ANSI across all commands
- **Non-interactive** errors: never prompt; return actionable error messages

## Implementation Notes
- Add `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default` helper (ru parity)
- Add `require_bash_4` helper (exit 3 on failure)
- Add `safe_path_under <root> <path>` and `safe_rm <path>`
- Use helpers in prune/build/install flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bash 4+ required with clear macOS guidance
- [ ] Relative paths rejected (with helpful error)
- [ ] Safe delete helper prevents accidental removal outside dsr dirs
- [ ] Temp dirs created only under `/tmp`
- [ ] All commands honor NO_COLOR and --no-color","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:53.109459246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:20.832616929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:20.832599226Z","close_reason":"Implemented guardrails.sh with all required functions. 22 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:53.109459246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jx","title":"E2E: dsr watch (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr watch (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate watch mode runs for a short interval using real GH API calls when credentials are available.

## Approach
- Use a short interval (e.g., 1-2s) and `--once` or a bounded loop.
- If gh auth is missing, **skip** with explicit guidance.
- Ensure stdout/stderr separation in --json mode.

## Logging
- Log start/end timestamps, interval, and command line.
- On failure, print stdout/stderr and last JSON payload.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watch runs a bounded cycle without hanging.
- [ ] --json output validates against schema.
- [ ] Skips are explicit when gh auth missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:10.936697092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:12:50.049182483Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:12:50.049164189Z","close_reason":"E2E watch tests complete: 11 tests (3 help, 5 preflight handling, 3 real auth). Tests already committed - verified all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jx","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:24.411408601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jx","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:26.191033314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nf","title":"Test infrastructure: structured logging for tests","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Structured Logging for Tests

## Purpose
Provide consistent, detailed logging infrastructure for all test files to improve debugging and CI visibility.

## Features

### 1. Log Levels
- DEBUG: Detailed step-by-step info
- INFO: Test progress and key events
- PASS: Successful assertions
- FAIL: Failed assertions with context
- SKIP: Skipped tests with reason

### 2. Output Formatting
```
[2026-01-30 12:34:56] [INFO] test_config.sh: Starting 12 tests
[2026-01-30 12:34:56] [DEBUG] test_init: Creating temp dir /tmp/dsr-test-xyz
[2026-01-30 12:34:57] [PASS] test_init: config_init creates directories
[2026-01-30 12:34:58] [FAIL] test_load: Expected 'info' got 'debug'
  -> File: test_config.sh:45
  -> Expected: log_level = info
  -> Actual: log_level = debug
```

### 3. File Output
- Per-test log files: `logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/test_*.log`
- Aggregated summary: `logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/summary.json`

### 4. Failure Capture
- Full stdout/stderr on failure
- Last N lines of relevant log
- Environment snapshot (key vars)

## API
```bash
source test_logging.sh
test_log_init "test_config.sh"
test_log_debug "Creating temp directory"
test_log_pass "config_init creates directories"
test_log_fail "Expected 'info' got 'debug'" "test_config.sh:45"
test_log_summary  # Print final results
```

## Integration
- Drop-in for existing test_*.sh files
- No external dependencies
- Works standalone and with test runner

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Consistent format across all tests
- [ ] Timestamps in ISO8601
- [ ] Failure context includes file:line
- [ ] Summary shows pass/fail/skip counts
- [ ] Works with run-all-tests.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.915636177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:34:14.872088355Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:34:14.872070150Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured test logging with 29 passing tests: log levels, ISO8601 timestamps, per-test logs, JSON summary, and assertion helpers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nf","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.915636177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-20l","title":"E2E: dsr fallback (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr fallback (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate the end-to-end fallback flow using real build and release paths when dependencies are available.

## Approach
- Use a minimal tool config and local repo fixture.
- If act/docker/gh auth missing, skip with explicit guidance.
- When deps available, run fallback in draft mode.

## Logging
- Capture per-step logs (check/build/release) and manifest output.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and build logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fallback completes end-to-end when deps available.
- [ ] Partial failure surfaces clear error and non-zero exit.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:04.180185543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:14:34.287609917Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:14:34.287592083Z","close_reason":"E2E fallback tests complete: 11 tests (9 pass, 2 conditional skip for config).  {"id":"bd-271","title":"Unit tests for config.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for config.sh Module

## Module Overview
config.sh provides XDG-compliant configuration management:
- `config_init` - Create default config files
- `config_load` - Load config into DSR_CONFIG array
- `config_get` - Retrieve config values with defaults
- `config_set` - Update config values
- `config_validate` - Schema validation
- `config_show` - Display configuration

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Initialization Tests
- `config_init` creates all required directories
- Creates default config.yaml with valid schema
- Creates hosts.yaml with default hosts
- Creates repos.yaml with empty registry
- Creates secrets/ directory with proper permissions (700)
- Does NOT overwrite existing config (unless --force)
- --force flag recreates config from scratch

### 2. Loading Tests
- `config_load` populates DSR_CONFIG associative array
- Missing config file triggers error with helpful message
- Malformed YAML produces clear error
- Default values applied for missing keys

### 3. Get/Set Tests
- `config_get` returns correct values
- `config_get` with default returns default when key missing
- `config_set` updates in-memory value
- `config_set --persist` writes to disk
- Nested key access (e.g., `signing.enabled`)

### 4. Validation Tests
- Valid config passes validation
- Invalid schema_version fails
- Missing required fields fail
- Type mismatches detected (string vs int)

### 5. XDG Compliance Tests
- Respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME override
- Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME override
- Respects XDG_STATE_HOME override
- Falls back to ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/state

## Test Approach
- Use TEMP_DIR for isolated testing
- Set DSR_CONFIG_DIR to temp directory
- Clean up after each test
- NO mocking - test real file operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:10:20.351497645Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:10:20.350701685Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-283","title":"E2E: dsr status (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr status (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify status output with real data sources (logs, cache, repos config) and JSON schema validity.

## Approach
- Run `dsr status` in a temp XDG state with seeded log files.
- If state is empty, ensure output is graceful and schema-valid.

## Logging
- Emit the temp XDG paths used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and relevant files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Human output includes last run summary when available.
- [ ] --json output matches schema and has empty stderr on success.
- [ ] Empty state handled without errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:22.561427223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:40.084311895Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:40.084293551Z","close_reason":"E2E status tests complete: 15 tests covering help, human output, JSON schema, empty state handling. Commit 595b524.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-283","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:28.014177245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-283","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:29.839327817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-29b","title":"E2E: dsr build (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr build (real behavior)

## Purpose
Exercise build command paths without mocks, using real local repos and dry-run/plan modes when external deps are missing.

## Approach
- Use a temp repo with a minimal workflow file for act.
- If docker/act missing, run `--dry-run` and assert planned actions.
- If docker/act available, execute a tiny workflow and verify artifacts.

## Logging
- Record repo path, workflow file, and tool config.
- On failure, dump act logs and stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] Real run (when deps available) produces artifacts + manifest.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:43.935502571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:32:35.613198159Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:32:35.613178973Z","close_reason":"Created e2e_build.sh with 14 tests (9 passing, 5 skipped due to yq dependency). Tests cover: help output, missing tool error handling, dry-run mode, specific target builds, and JSON output validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:35.186637406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:37.023320756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -1108,9 +1108,7 @@ act_run_native_build() {
 
         # Handle Windows specifics
         if [[ "$platform" == windows/* ]]; then
-            # Convert forward slashes to backslashes for Windows
-            remote_artifact_path="${remote_artifact_path//\//\\}"
-            # Add .exe extension
+            # Add .exe extension (keep forward slashes - SCP via OpenSSH uses them)
             remote_artifact_path+=".exe"
         fi
